articles.title: U.S. Warns China Over Iranian Oil as Sanctions Fight Intensifies
articles.summary: China told its independent refineries to disregard U.S. sanctions over their purchases of Iranian crude.

articles.title: Met Gala 2026 Red Carpet Photos: All the Looks
articles.summary: See the arrivals on the cobblestone carpet.

articles.title: Trump Administration Demands Names of 2020 Election Workers in Georgia
articles.summary: The Justice Department again appears to be using the investigative power of the federal government to rehash debunked claims that Democrats stole the 2020 election.

articles.title: Trump Tries to Downplay Economic Effects of the Iran War
articles.summary: At a White House event for Small Business Week, he described the economy as “roaring” and predicted that gas prices would go down soon.

articles.title: Attacks Threaten to Reignite Iran Conflict
articles.summary: Also, the stars arrive at the Met Gala. Here’s the latest at the end of Monday.

articles.title: See the looks from the 2026 Met Gala red carpet
articles.summary: Extravagantly dressed celebrities and designers made their way up the famous Met staircase Monday evening. The fundraiser is one of the fashion industry's biggest nights.

articles.title: Soil at D.C. Golf Course Where East Wing Debris Was Dumped Contains Toxic Metals
articles.summary: The National Park Service said the dump of debris, between the fourth and ninth holes, does not exceed environmental limits. Opponents disagree.

articles.title: White House Considers Vetting A.I. Models Before They Are Released
articles.summary: The Trump administration, which took a noninterventionist approach to artificial intelligence, is now discussing imposing oversight on A.I. models before they are made publicly available.

articles.title: Hotels have a big World Cup problem: Bookings are running far below projections
articles.summary: Nearly 80% of hotels surveyed by a body representing the industry reported bookings were running below projections — a worrisome sign with only weeks to go before the World Cup.

articles.title: Why U.S. troops are stationed in Germany, and what happens if some leave
articles.summary: NPR's Mary Louise Kelly talks with Jeff Rathke, president of the American-German Institute, about U.S. troops stationed in Germany, and what happens if President Trump moves some of them elsewhere.

articles.title: Modi's party takes control of India's West Bengal in key state election
articles.summary: The outcome is expected to strengthen Modi's political position midway through his third term.

articles.title: Here are the 2026 Pulitzer Prize winners
articles.summary: Award winners include authors Daniel Kraus, Jill Lepore and Yiyun Li, opinion writer M. Gessen and staffers and contributors at The Washington Post, Reuters and AP.

articles.title: Supreme Court Temporarily Restores Access to Abortion Pill by Mail
articles.summary: A lower-court ruling had reinstated a Food and Drug Administration requirement that patients visit a health care provider in person to obtain mifepristone.

articles.title: A United jet struck a light pole and a truck near Newark airport, police say
articles.summary: New Jersey State Police say a United Airlines passenger jet struck a light pole and damaged a truck as it was coming in for a landing at Newark Liberty International Airport on Sunday.

articles.title: Supreme Court gives abortion pill mifepristone a 1-week reprieve from a major change
articles.summary: The Supreme Court says rules that allow patients to get the abortion pill mifepristone through the mail can stay the same for at least a week.

articles.title: The U.S. fights to reopen the Strait of Hormuz as UAE says it's attacked by Iran
articles.summary: The U.S. military said it battled Iranian forces and sank six small boats as it moved to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, while the United Arab Emirates said Iran fired missiles and drones its way.

articles.title: The many ways Trump wants to change D.C., from buildings to statues to parks
articles.summary: Many of the changes Trump wants to make to the White House and the city are facing legal challenges. Some are reversible, while others could reshape the nation's capital for generations to come.

articles.title: Trump says U.S. will 'guide' stranded ships through the Strait. And, key Senate races to watch
articles.summary: President Trump announced that the U.S will "guide" stranded ships through the Strait of Hormuz. And, Democrats face an uphill battle to gain Senate control. Here are the key races to watch.

articles.title: Rescuers help Timmy, a stranded whale, return home
articles.summary: Rescuers defy warnings as the stranded "Humpback Hope," also known in German media as Timmy, is carried by barge into the North Sea, racing against time to save her.

articles.title: 2 U.S. service members missing after military exercises in Morocco
articles.summary: Two U.S. service members are missing in southwestern Morocco after taking part in annual multinational military exercises in the North African country, the United States Africa Command (AFRICOM) said.

articles.title: Trump says the U.S. will 'guide' stranded ships from the Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: The United States will launch an effort on Monday to "guide" stranded ships from the Iran-gripped Strait of Hormuz, President Donald Trump said, as two ships around the strait reported attacks.

articles.title: Hantavirus outbreak kills 3 on cruise ship in the Atlantic Ocean, WHO says
articles.summary: Hantaviruses are usually spread by exposure to urine, saliva or feces from infected rodents, such as rats or mice. Hantavirus infections are rare but can cause deadly respiratory infections.

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
